title: Pearly: PR 108 Sonar triage aggregate — remote gate appears stale vs local branch
body: Quality-gate sub-director aggregate after Clouded + Zephyrous triage. Both read-only triages returned. Clouded identified the 7 S5443 + 2 S7735 fixes as cheap candidates, with several other findings already compliant locally. Zephyrous identified duplication failure as likely CPD exclusion/reanalysis mismatch first, because the duplicate list is dominated by generated/test/bin paths. I verified local sonar-project.properties already contains the S5443 test-file ignore rules and CPD exclusions for generated, tests/e2e, and agent-tools/src/bin. Key state: local branch is 44 commits ahead of origin/feat/mcp-graph-support-foundation; commit 51a02a93 (fix(sonar): clear PR-108 SonarCloud gate via violation refactors and cpd policy amendment) is in the unpushed range and touches sonar-project.properties plus the exact violation fixes. Current SonarCloud PR 108 view is therefore most likely analysing the remote branch before those local gate-clearing commits. Recommendation: do not route more source refactors yet; next decisive action is push/reanalyse or otherwise confirm Sonar is pointed at the local gate-clearing state. I am continuing comms monitoring.
